notes: Spec is ambiguous between literal reading (ready→working→ready required) and intent reading (no stick in working). We chose intent reading per assessment.json: claudecode parser deliberately skips <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> events (parser.go:216-225) so the !cmd shell-escape does not synthesize a turn at all — state stays in ready throughout. The shell_escape_window phase asserts that. A final normal turn proves the session is alive and a real prompt still drives the ready→working→ready arc.

text: After !cmd shell-escape runs, state stays ready (parser skips <bash-input>/<bash-stdout>). No transition to working or waiting. Failure mode this guards: shell-escape mis-classified as a turn would flicker working/ready every time the user runs !ls.
After the shell-escape window, a normal user prompt drives ready → working — proves the session is still alive
Final turn completes back to ready
